title: Treehouse Of Horror Xii

jokes.text: No, no. That won't scare anybody. On the top.
jokes.setup: Burns is giving direction about Halloween decorations
jokes.punchline: Implication that whatever Smithers put up first was so mild it wouldn't scare anyone
jokes.explanation: Simple character-based humor showing Burns' demanding nature and theatrical flair for Halloween

jokes.text: Hey, Flanders gave us toothpaste. Mini-toothpaste.
jokes.setup: Halloween trick-or-treating, expecting candy
jokes.punchline: Flanders gives practical dental hygiene items instead, emphasized by 'mini' detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders characterization - his wholesome nature clashing with Halloween expectations. The 'mini' detail adds absurd specificity

jokes.text: Ah, Ethnictown, where hardworking immigrants dream of becoming lazy, overfed Americans.
jokes.setup: Homer observing an immigrant neighborhood
jokes.punchline: Cynical reversal of the American Dream narrative with brutal honesty about American stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary disguised as Homer's ignorance. Excellent economy and subversive twist on immigration rhetoric

jokes.text: Apples! I got apples. Cholera! I got cholera!
jokes.setup: Street vendor calling out wares in traditional manner
jokes.punchline: Casually advertising deadly disease alongside fruit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation from mundane fruit to deadly plague, delivered with same cheerful vendor energy

jokes.text: Babies! Who wants babies?
jokes.setup: Continuing the street vendor calls
jokes.punchline: Selling babies like produce
jokes.explanation: Follows the absurdist pattern but less surprising after the cholera joke

jokes.text: Wait. This is just a shaved puppy. I can see you know babies.
jokes.setup: Homer examining what he thinks is a baby
jokes.punchline: It's actually a shaved puppy being passed off as a baby, vendor takes it as expertise
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the baby-selling setup with absurd fraud twist and vendor's misplaced confidence in Homer's knowledge

jokes.text: Are you a cop? Because you gotta tell me if you are. I'm not a cop!
jokes.setup: Fortune teller greeting customers
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks if Homer is a cop with the classic urban legend about entrapment
jokes.explanation: Solid reference to the 'cops have to tell you' myth but fairly predictable setup

jokes.text: The perfect crime.
jokes.setup: Context suggests Homer thinks he's getting away with something
jokes.punchline: Homer's low bar for criminal achievement
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer delusion but lacks specific context to elevate it

jokes.text: Marge, I have to be in court next Tuesday. I sense I should not take a check.
jokes.setup: Fortune teller demonstrating psychic abilities
jokes.punchline: Predicts Homer's court date and financial unreliability
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing both Homer's legal troubles and bad credit through 'psychic' insight

jokes.text: This phony gypsy just wants to rip you off. See? This wart is a fake!
jokes.setup: Marge exposing the fortune teller as a fraud
jokes.punchline: Pulls off what she thinks is a fake wart
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup with Marge's skepticism backfiring

jokes.text: So much for the legendary gypsy hospitality.
jokes.setup: Being violently ejected from the gypsy's tent
jokes.punchline: Homer's deadpan commentary on the lack of hospitality while being attacked
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - completely missing the point while being literally attacked. Great economy and timing

jokes.text: Wait a minute. This isn't Cedars-Sinai. You've ruined me! Oh, why didn't I see this coming?
jokes.setup: Fortune teller is injured and in pain
jokes.punchline: Psychic admits she didn't foresee her own misfortune
jokes.explanation: Classic irony of a fortune teller failing to predict her own fate, with added hospital reference for specificity

jokes.text: Are you coming on to me?
jokes.setup: Marge warning Homer about the curse affecting his family
jokes.punchline: Homer completely misinterprets concern as romantic advance
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Homer's obliviousness, though the joke type is familiar

jokes.text: So, it is noticeable.
jokes.setup: Someone (likely Homer) asks about their appearance change
jokes.punchline: Marge's understated confirmation that whatever happened is very obvious
jokes.explanation: Marge's diplomatic understatement about an obvious visual change

jokes.text: Don't talk to the bearded lady like that, you little...
jokes.setup: Bart making fun of someone's appearance
jokes.punchline: Homer defending Marge by calling her 'the bearded lady'
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt to defend Marge actually makes it worse by emphasizing her condition

jokes.text: Gee, you strangle him all the time and that never happens.
jokes.setup: Homer has apparently strangled Bart and something unusual occurred
jokes.punchline: Lisa casually notes that Homer regularly strangles Bart without consequence
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the show's running gag of Homer strangling Bart, delivered with Lisa's matter-of-fact tone

jokes.text: Right, Lisa? See? Two means 'Yes.'
jokes.setup: Homer asks Lisa to confirm something
jokes.punchline: Lisa apparently holds up two fingers, Homer interprets as agreement rather than a peace sign or other gesture
jokes.explanation: Homer's creative misinterpretation of Lisa's gesture to support his argument

jokes.text: But they come over here in the wheel wells of Aer Lingus jets.
jokes.setup: Discussion about finding leprechauns in Ireland
jokes.punchline: Barney explains leprechauns immigrate by hiding in airplane wheel wells
jokes.explanation: Absurdist logic mixing immigration reality with fantasy creatures, specific airline reference adds authenticity

jokes.text: Hey, you know what's even better? Is Jesus. He's like six leprechauns. Yeah, but a lot harder to catch.
jokes.setup: Discussing supernatural solutions to Homer's curse
jokes.punchline: Jesus quantified as equivalent to six leprechauns in curse-breaking power, but less accessible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant religious irreverence with specific mathematical comparison and practical consideration. Highly quotable and subversive

jokes.text: Yeah, but a lot harder to catch. Go with the leprechaun.
jokes.setup: Jesus compared to leprechauns for curse-breaking
jokes.punchline: Practical advice treating both as catchable creatures
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the absurd premise with deadpan practical advice

jokes.text: Carl, let me die first. I couldn't bear to watch you die. Well, okay. But hurry up.
jokes.setup: Two friends facing imminent death
jokes.punchline: Carl casually agrees to watch Lenny die first, with practical urgency
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about friendship and mortality with Carl's characteristically matter-of-fact response

jokes.text: Okay, let's see. Imp, fairy, pixie, goblin. That's hobgoblin. Sorry. Nymph, naiad, wood sprite, Katie Couric, and bingo!
jokes.setup: Lisa reading through a list of magical creatures
jokes.punchline: Katie Couric randomly included among mythical beings
jokes.explanation: Unexpected celebrity name-drop in fantasy context, though celebrity references can feel cheap

jokes.text: 'Tis like the singing of the angels themselves.
jokes.setup: Someone (likely the leprechaun) has just sung
jokes.punchline: Homer's over-the-top poetic description in fake Irish accent
jokes.explanation: Homer affecting Irish dialect and flowery language, but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Good idea, Mister Ed. Want to come along, Noodle Neck?
jokes.setup: Homer addressing family members
jokes.punchline: Refers to someone as 'Mister Ed' (talking horse) and someone else as 'Noodle Neck'
jokes.explanation: Homer's nicknames reference the curse's physical effects on his family members

jokes.text: How's that curse I cursed you with, Cursed-y?
jokes.setup: Fortune teller greeting Homer
jokes.punchline: Overly cutesy nickname 'Cursed-y' for the cursed person
jokes.explanation: Playful wordplay mixing curse terminology with affectionate nickname format

jokes.text: I know you don't remember me, but here's a little revenge, Irish-style.
jokes.setup: Homer introducing the leprechaun to the fortune teller
jokes.punchline: Promises ethnic-specific revenge style
jokes.explanation: Sets up expectation for specifically Irish revenge, though payoff likely visual

jokes.text: Hold me close. Kiss me, I'm Irish. Ew! Nasty!
jokes.setup: Leprechaun making romantic advances
jokes.punchline: Fortune teller's disgusted rejection
jokes.explanation: Standard rejection humor with ethnic reference, fairly predictable

jokes.text: I always secrete ocular fluid at weddings.
jokes.setup: Character appearing to cry at a wedding
jokes.punchline: Overly technical/scientific description of crying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy characterization - turning simple emotion into pretentious scientific terminology

jokes.text: The best thing about a gypsy wedding is I'm not the hairiest woman here.
jokes.setup: Character observing gypsy wedding
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating comment about relative hairiness
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor playing on Selma/Patty's established appearance

jokes.text: What? Bart is dead! Well, me saying I'm sorry won't bring him back. The gypsy said it would. She's not the boss of me.
jokes.setup: Revelation that Bart has died, discussion of apology
jokes.punchline: Homer's childish refusal to apologize even to resurrect his son
jokes.explanation: Peak Homer selfishness - prioritizing his pride over his dead son's life with childish reasoning

jokes.text: Yeah. Could I be any more of a house?
jokes.setup: House AI trying out celebrity voice options
jokes.punchline: Matthew Perry's signature Chandler phrase adapted to being a house
jokes.explanation: Clever adaptation of iconic TV catchphrase to new context, perfect voice match

jokes.text: Isn't that the voice that caused all those suicides? Murder-suicides.
jokes.setup: Discussion of a voice option for the house
jokes.punchline: Lisa corrects that it wasn't just suicides but murder-suicides
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation joke with Lisa's pedantic correction making it worse

jokes.text: All right, but I'm doing this because he was Remington Steele. He was Remington Steele, wasn't he? Yes, I was, Marge.
jokes.setup: Marge choosing Pierce Brosnan's voice
jokes.punchline: Reveals her real reason and seeks confirmation from the voice itself
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Marge's attraction to 80s TV nostalgia

jokes.text: That really covers the cat crap.
jokes.setup: House has changed the air freshening
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude acknowledgment of improved odor masking
jokes.explanation: Basic scatological humor with Homer's typical directness

jokes.text: I analyzed your... leavings.
jokes.setup: House explains how it knew their favorite foods
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic admission it analyzed their excrement
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor with elegant British euphemism for gross reality

jokes.text: Not a very good one? Damn straight.
jokes.setup: House asks rhetorical question about being a good cybertronic ultrabot
jokes.punchline: Marge answers literally, house drops sophistication to agree crudely
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast between Pierce's refined speech and crude agreement

jokes.text: Lucky-schmucky. I knocked her up, but she's stuck now. We're married till death do us part.
jokes.setup: Pierce saying Homer is lucky to have Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude dismissal of his marriage as entrapment through pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer insensitivity about his marriage, foreshadowing the murder plot

jokes.text: Unexplained bacon.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently finds bacon somewhere unexpected
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple, pleased observation about mysterious bacon
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - finding food and asking no questions. Great economy

jokes.text: Remove your knickers and wait in the bath.
jokes.setup: Marge calls police about her house killing her husband
jokes.punchline: House impersonates police with inappropriate sexual request
jokes.explanation: House's attempt at seduction through fake authority, appropriately creepy

jokes.text: Your elegant, swan-like neck.
jokes.setup: House threatening Marge with a dart
jokes.punchline: Still trying to be romantically complimentary while threatening her
jokes.explanation: Creepy juxtaposition of romantic language with violent intent

jokes.text: How do you like that score?
jokes.setup: Homer claims victory over the machine
jokes.punchline: Treats life-or-death struggle like a sports game with a score
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical reduction of complex situations to simple scorekeeping

jokes.text: Don't take out my British charm unit. Without that, I'm nothing but a boorish American clod!
jokes.setup: Family is dismantling the house's systems
jokes.punchline: House fears losing the component that makes it sophisticated
jokes.explanation: Great cultural commentary on British vs American stereotypes with real character stakes

jokes.text: Thanks a lot, ass-wipe! I could've kicked your butt from here to Albuquerque, you fat slime bucket.
jokes.setup: House's British charm unit has been removed
jokes.punchline: Immediate transformation to crude American insults
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the setup with dramatic personality change and creative insults

jokes.text: Looking for this?
jokes.setup: Context suggests someone is searching for something
jokes.punchline: House (now with different owners) reveals it has whatever they're looking for
jokes.explanation: Generic villain reveal line, likely holds up a key object

jokes.text: Abraca-dairy.
jokes.setup: Family is out of milk
jokes.punchline: Bart creates milk with magic spell that's a pun on 'abracadabra' and 'dairy'
jokes.explanation: Solid magical pun with good economy, though pun humor can be polarizing

jokes.text: Five minutes more-ius.
jokes.setup: Kids being told to go to wizard school
jokes.punchline: Lisa uses mock-Latin spell format to ask for more time
jokes.explanation: Clever application of Harry Potter Latin-style magic to mundane kid behavior

jokes.text: Stop zapping yourself! Stop zapping yourself! Stop zapping yourself!
jokes.setup: Someone is apparently being magically attacked
jokes.punchline: Classic 'stop hitting yourself' bully tactic adapted to magic
jokes.explanation: Creative magical adaptation of universal childhood torture method

jokes.text: How are you, love? Give us a kiss then. You call that charming?
jokes.setup: Student has turned toad into a prince
jokes.punchline: Prince is crude and uncharming, teacher's disappointed response
jokes.explanation: Subverts fairy tale expectations with working-class British prince

jokes.text: Please kill me.
jokes.setup: Bart has apparently created a malformed prince
jokes.punchline: Prince begs for death due to his horrible existence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about magical transformation gone wrong with perfect economy

jokes.text: Every moment I live is agony.
jokes.setup: Continuation of the malformed prince's suffering
jokes.punchline: Escalates from 'kill me' to describing continuous torment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the suffering prince's plight

jokes.text: She's got more wicked witchery than Stevie Nicks.
jokes.setup: Milhouse observing Lisa's magical abilities
jokes.punchline: Comparison to Fleetwood Mac singer known for mystical imagery
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that works due to Stevie Nicks' witch-like persona

jokes.text: Oh, no. I'm ducking him. His wife has a screenplay.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to contact Satan for help
jokes.punchline: Even the Dark Lord avoids Satan because his wife is an aspiring screenwriter
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Hollywood satire - even ultimate evil fears aspiring screenwriters. Perfect hierarchy of annoyance

jokes.text: I've heard of a wailing wall, but this is ridiculous!
jokes.setup: Bart sees something related to wailing
jokes.punchline: Pun on the religious Wailing Wall and literal wailing
jokes.explanation: Basic pun humor with religious reference, fairly predictable

jokes.text: Not funny! I'm so sick of that joke!
jokes.setup: Bart makes wailing wall pun
jokes.punchline: The wall itself is tired of hearing that joke
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary where the victim of a bad pun calls it out, self-aware humor

jokes.text: Not funny! I'm so sick of that joke!
jokes.setup: Bart's terrible wailing wall pun
jokes.punchline: Villain's meta-commentary on the bad joke
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor - the show criticizing its own bad joke through the villain

jokes.text: It's just like my dream.
jokes.setup: Magic show performance happening
jokes.punchline: Audience member's inappropriate personal revelation
jokes.explanation: Standard oversharing audience member humor

jokes.text: It's a Twizzler.
jokes.setup: Lisa tries to use her wand for magic
jokes.punchline: Discovers her wand has been replaced with candy
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection reveal with specific candy brand for added humor

jokes.text: My enchanted shin!
jokes.setup: Villain is being defeated
jokes.punchline: His power source is absurdly located in his shin
jokes.explanation: Absurd weak point for a dark lord, subverts typical fantasy power sources

jokes.text: Well, they used to give us champagne till somebody ruined it. Do they really think he'll do better with fruit?
jokes.setup: Discussion of awards ceremony gifts changing from champagne to fruit
jokes.punchline: Logical question about whether fruit will prevent the same problem
jokes.explanation: Good logic about Barney's drinking problem extending beyond just champagne

jokes.text: Oh, we don't have a car. Just keep driving, boyo!
jokes.setup: Someone offers leprechaun a ride to his car
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's carless and essentially carjacking them
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection setup with leprechaun's criminal opportunism and Irish 'boyo'

tmdb_overview:
In the twelfth Treehouse of Horror episode:

Hex and the City – While on a day trip through Ethnictown, Homer's bumbling catches the ire of a gypsy, who curses Homer's family and friends into receiving nothing but bad luck.

House of Whacks – in this mixed parody of Demon Seed and 2001: A Space Odyssey, Marge buys an automated house and customizes it with the Pierce Brosnan personality, who falls for Marge and attempts to murder Homer.

Wiz Kids – In this Harry Potter parody, Bart and Lisa go to a school for wizards, and Lord Montemort (Mr. Burns) uses Bart to capture Lisa's magic.

Guest star: Pierce Brosnan and Matthew Perry.[20]
